ModuleName: Applied Data Structures: Blockchain, Digital Currency, and Beyond

Artifacts.Name: Module Topics
Artifacts.Description: Outlines the module's subject matter across three areas—foundational data structures (hash tables, trees, graphs), blockchain architecture (linked lists, cryptographic hashing, Merkle trees), and digital currency systems (wallets, UTXO model)—giving learners and instructors a structured overview of the topics to be studied.

Artifacts.Name: Module Topics (HTML)
Artifacts.Description: A topic outline covering how core data structures—hash tables, trees, and graphs—underpin real-world systems like blockchain and digital currency, giving learners and instructors a conceptual roadmap of the module's progression from foundational concepts to applied use cases.

Artifacts.Name: Foundations of Data Structures in Real-World Systems
Artifacts.Description: A reading that introduces foundational data structures (such as arrays, linked lists, trees, and hash tables) and explains how they underpin real-world computing systems like social networks, file systems, and blockchains, serving as a conceptual starting point for learners entering the module.

Artifacts.Name: Blockchain Architecture and Data Structures
Artifacts.Description: A reading that explains how blockchain technology is constructed from linked lists, cryptographic hash functions, and Merkle trees to create a tamper-resistant distributed ledger, helping learners understand the computer science principles behind blockchain architecture.

Artifacts.Name: Digital Currency Systems
Artifacts.Description: A reading that covers how digital currencies like Bitcoin and Ethereum use data structures and cryptography to achieve decentralized, tamper-resistant transaction records, enabling learners to understand the technical foundations of cryptocurrency systems.

Artifacts.Name: Graphs and Social Network Applications
Artifacts.Description: A reading that explains how social networks are modeled as graphs, covers key graph data structures and traversal algorithms, and shows how these are applied to real-world problems such as friend recommendations and network analysis.

Artifacts.Name: Combining Data Structures in Complex Systems
Artifacts.Description: A reading that examines how complex software systems combine multiple data structures together, explains the reasoning behind those design choices, and analyzes the trade-offs and consequences of good versus poor structural decisions, helping learners think holistically about system design.

Artifacts.Name: Combining Data Structures in Complex Systems
Artifacts.Description: A reading that examines how complex software systems combine multiple data structures together, explains the reasoning behind those design choices, and analyzes the trade-offs and consequences of good versus poor structural decisions, helping learners think holistically about system design.
